content: Current discussions often frame Self-Refine as a structural framework and LLM self-reflection mechanisms as core functional components, but we can reverse this relationship: the iterative self-feedback loop of the Self-Refine framework can act as a meta-structural organizer that activates and chains multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives from LLM self-reflection mechanisms, eliminating the need for both external supervision and external test cases entirely. This creates a fully endogenous self-improvement loop for LLMs where structural iteration resolves the inconsistency between multiple reflective perspectives to drive output quality gains.
